objective: Authorize a closed Work-Issue only for an exact checker-validated capability-batch done-to-done corrective PR without weakening the default open-issue requirement.
claim_ceiling: Accepted E0 offline PR-metadata correction only: a closed Work-Issue may pass required metadata validation only for an exact owned capability-batch done-to-done corrective advance with identical valid accepted, target, terminal, and issue identities. A contiguous blocked Gate 0 v7 successor preserves frozen v6 and every artifact, attestation, blocker, and readiness state. This does not accept D9, authorize a D4 handoff or D8 implementation, reopen any issue, or permit source, scheduler, runtime, release, database, consumer, or Production data work.

requirements: the queued registration is merged on main before this preparation revision pins the activation base, independent reviewer, and frozen Local/CI corpus
the E0 implementation installs a content-addressed integration lease before changing the shared delivery validator, tests, or Gate 0 successor files
the implementation recognizes only an exact capability-batch done-to-done advance whose base and candidate issue and terminal rung identities match the exported closed Work-Issue
open issues retain existing behavior while closed standalone, registration, preparation, normal advancement, mismatched-issue, malformed, invalid-rung, and rung-changing cases remain rejected
the implementation preserves Work-Key, Issue-Action, closing-keyword, changed-path, integration-lease, lifecycle, and review requirements
Gate 0 v6 remains byte-for-byte frozen and a contiguous blocked v7 successor preserves every accepted artifact, external attestation, blocker, and readiness state

later_claim_dependencies: D9 #274 may rely on an accepted D11 E0 result only to make its explicit local terminal-rerun preflight option usable by required PR governance; D11 does not accept D9.
D4 #210 still requires a separate checker-authorized done-to-done corrective handoff PR, and D8 #271 remains independently gated.

required_strata: an open Work-Issue remains accepted under the existing metadata rules
a closed Work-Issue is accepted only for an exact owned capability-batch done-to-done corrective advance
closed standalone, registration, preparation, active, and normal terminal-acceptance advances are rejected
closed mismatched-issue, malformed-manifest, invalid-rung, and rung-changing corrective candidates are rejected
Work-Key, Issue-Action, closing-keyword, changed-path, lease, lifecycle, and review controls remain mandatory

source_ceiling: No network source, credential, Vault, database, object-store, scheduler, runtime, release, consumer, or Production data operation is authorized.

release_activation.reason: D11 changes only offline pull-request authorization metadata and cannot activate a source, runtime, schedule, consumer, release, or data flow.

negative_controls: any closed Work-Issue outside an exact owned capability-batch done-to-done corrective advance
a closed standalone, registration, preparation, active, normal terminal-acceptance, mismatched-issue, malformed, invalid-rung, or rung-changing candidate
bypassing Work-Key, Issue-Action, closing-keyword, changed-path, integration-lease, lifecycle, or review validation
mutating Gate 0 v6 bytes, accepted artifacts, external attestations, blockers, or readiness while rebinding the delivery validator
reopening an issue or changing D9, D4, D8, source policy, scheduler, runtime, release, database, consumer, or Production data authorization

allowed_evidence_readers: D9 #274 terminal-preflight acceptance only; no D4 handoff, D8 implementation, source, runtime, release, or Production authorization

invalidation: Changing the closed-issue predicate, accepted transition class, rung vocabulary, Work-Issue identity binding, Gate 0 successor binding, path authorization, or corpus requires a new prepared revision before implementation.

Any attempt to combine this validator repair with D9 implementation, a D4 handoff, D8 implementation, source access, scheduler, runtime, release, database, consumer, or data-path work requires separate authorization.
rollback: Revert only the future D11 PR-metadata predicate, tests, Gate 0 successor, and E0 evidence; do not mutate D9, D4, D8, issue lifecycle state, source policy, runtime, release, database, consumer, or historical governance evidence.
